security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote data can be modified without detection.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not protect against manipulation of vote data.

assumptions: The governance contract does not validate vote data integrity.
The actor can modify the vote data before submission.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic hashing and integrity checks for vote data.

description: An attacker may alter the vote data to influence the outcome of the proposal.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes are not linked to user identities in a verifiable way.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system lacks mechanisms to ensure accountability for votes.

assumptions: The system does not use mechanisms like digital signatures or verifiable credentials.
The actor can cast votes anonymously.

mitigation.strategy: Use a secure, auditable voting system with user authentication and non-repudiation mechanisms.

description: An attacker may cast votes and deny involvement, making it difficult to trace back their actions.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote details are exposed to unauthorized parties.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not protect vote confidentiality.

assumptions: The governance contract exposes vote details publicly.
The actor can access the exposed vote data.

mitigation.strategy: Implement confidentiality mechanisms to restrict access to vote details.

description: An attacker may gain access to vote data, potentially revealing voting patterns or user preferences.
